ops.text: METHODOLOGICAL FINDING (E43 lane, self-caught + fixed): session-disjointness is NOT sufficient for leak-free splits — two DISTINCT session-disjoint moments can render BYTE-IDENTICAL steer_type_text. The lane's verify_split gate caught 1 traces/train row byte-identical to 1 frozen-250 eval_a row (0 session overlap). Fixed prep.py to exclude any train/gold_train row whose EXACT text appears in any eval set → TRAIN 3348→3347, eval_a/eval_b/gold_train digests unchanged, verify_split.json session_and_text_disjoint=true, all overlaps zero; baselines re-fit + 3-seed encoder training restarted on the clean set. GENERAL LESSON for the instrument card v2 (E30.6): the frozen-frame freeze rule should assert TEXT-disjointness across splits, not just session-hash disjointness — near_dup_representatives dedups within a frame but exact cross-split text dups can survive a session split. Bounding note on already-run experiments: E32 (many-shot) is robust (it was a NEGATIVE; any text leak would only have helped the losing arm); E36's encoder-vs-incumbent comparison is relative so any shared leak affects all arms roughly equally. Flag for card v2, not a re-run of prior experiments.
